originalAlarm.description: `eckey` could be null (null value originating from line 10) and is dereferenced.

model:
(define-fun SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto () Bool
  false)
(define-fun HAVE_ECC () Bool
  true)
reasoning: The known VBDB bug for 35ae2e259e4.c is the memory leak of eckey (PRODUCT_30: TRUE, unix.Malloc). This alarm detects a null dereference: eckey = malloc() at L10 can return NULL, and *eckey = 30 at L12 dereferences it — a genuine new bug. The null dereference at L12 is inside the if(rand()%2) branch; SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto only affects the else branch (L14-18) and has no bearing on whether L12 is reached or whether eckey is NULL there. The bug exists for all HAVE_ECC=T configs regardless of SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto. The PC unnecessarily includes \!SSL_CTX_set_ecdh_auto, making it over-constrained (too narrow).
